body.title: CLOSED: /cognition-debug skill — realtime single-step of Igor's cognition cycle
body.result: Skill file written: /cognition-debug — replay + realtime modes, MCP tool wrappers, diagnosis table, engram note.
body.description:
CC skill that single-steps Igor's cognition cycle (input → thalamus → action-selection → basal_ganglia → NE → response) in realtime, pausing between phases to inspect actual state. Wraps existing MCP tools (hot_nodes, hot_attractors, twm_read, traces_get, turn_trace_recent, tail_heat, wg_neighbors) into a guided stepping interface. Same motivation as /debug-pe-chain: post-analysis requires assumptions; stepping exposes actual state at each boundary.

Covers two modes:
1. **Realtime** — pause at each cognition phase boundary during a live turn (requires Igor to support a step-mode flag or breakpoint protocol)
2. **Replay** — step through a recorded turn_trace after the fact, showing what each phase saw and decided

Engram execution is in scope — when a habit fires, the skill shows which engram ran and what its output was (requires T-engram-logging-primitive to be shipped first for full coverage).

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/skills/ (new skill: cognition_debug.md)
**Design rules:** SWADL. Skill wraps existing MCP tools — no new Igor internals required for basic replay mode. Realtime mode may need a breakpoint hook (companion ticket if needed).
**Scope boundary:** IN: skill file, replay mode using existing trace tools, engram step display (gated on T-engram-logging-primitive). OUT: new MCP verbs (that's T-mcp-igor-cognition-debug-capability), modifying Igor's turn loop.
**Test plan:** step through a recorded turn_trace for a known turn (e.g. the 2026-05-03 20:17:27 misroute incident); verify each phase output is surfaced correctly.
